meta.name: Shadowrun 6th Edition
meta.description: Cyberpunk meets fantasy. Assemble dice pools of d6s; every 5 or 6 is a hit. More hits = better outcome.

dice.name: Dice Pool
dice.description: Assemble a pool of d6s equal to attribute + skill. Count dice showing 5 or 6 as hits. More hits = better outcome. A glitch occurs when more than half your dice show 1s.

attributes.label: Body
attributes.description: Measures physical toughness, resistance to damage, and the ability to endure hardship.

attributes.label: Agility
attributes.description: Measures physical coordination, fine motor control, and ranged combat ability.

attributes.label: Reaction
attributes.description: Measures reflexes and the ability to respond quickly to changing situations.

attributes.label: Strength
attributes.description: Measures raw physical power and melee combat prowess.

attributes.label: Willpower
attributes.description: Represents force of personality and resistance to mental and magical manipulation.

attributes.label: Logic
attributes.description: Measures analytical ability, rational thought, and technical know-how.

attributes.label: Intuition
attributes.description: Represents awareness, gut feelings, and the ability to read situations.

attributes.label: Charisma
attributes.description: Measures force of personality, social grace, and ability to lead or persuade.

attributes.label: Edge
attributes.description: Luck and street smarts. Spent to gain advantages; refreshes between sessions.

attributes.label: Essence
attributes.description: Measures how much of your soul remains. Reduced by cyberware. Affects Magic and Resonance.

attributes.label: Magic
attributes.description: Required to cast spells or perform adept powers. Limited by Essence.

attributes.label: Resonance
attributes.description: Required to interact with the Matrix as a Technomancer. Limited by Essence.

skills.label: Athletics
skills.description: Climbing, running, swimming, and jumping. The catch-all for physical challenges.

skills.label: Stealth
skills.description: Moving silently, hiding, and avoiding detection.

skills.label: Firearms
skills.description: Using pistols, rifles, shotguns, and other conventional projectile weapons.

skills.label: Close Combat
skills.description: Melee fighting with blades, clubs, and unarmed techniques.

skills.label: Cracking
skills.description: Hacking, defeating IC, and intruding into hosts. The decker's core offensive skill.

skills.label: Electronics
skills.description: Repairing and operating electronic devices; includes hardware and software knowledge.

skills.label: Engineering
skills.description: Building, repairing, and modifying mechanical and structural items.

skills.label: Piloting
skills.description: Operating ground vehicles, aircraft, and watercraft.

skills.label: Perception
skills.description: Noticing things in your environment; the most commonly tested skill in the game.

skills.label: Con
skills.description: Deceiving people, running scams, and impersonation.

skills.label: Negotiation
skills.description: Making deals, haggling prices, and reaching agreements.

skills.label: Intimidation
skills.description: Coercing others through threat of harm or personal menace.

derived.label: Initiative
derived.description: Base initiative score (plus 1d6 standard, more dice with cyberware/drugs/magic).

derived.label: Composure
derived.description: Threshold to resist fear, shock, and emotional manipulation.

derived.label: Judge Intentions
derived.description: Used to sense if someone is lying or has hostile intent.

derived.label: Memory
derived.description: How much detail a character can recall under stress.

derived.label: Lift / Carry
derived.description: Threshold for lifting heavy objects or carrying loads across distances.

resources.label: Physical Monitor
resources.description: Boxes of physical damage. Fill all of them and you're incapacitated (or dead).

resources.label: Stun Monitor
resources.description: Boxes of stun damage. Fill these and you're knocked out.

resources.label: Edge Pool
resources.description: Current Edge available to spend. Refreshes at defined intervals.

steps.label: Concept & Metatype
steps.hint: Who is your runner? Human, elf, dwarf, ork, or troll — each has different attribute maximums.

fields.label: Runner Archetype (e.g. Street Samurai, Decker, Mage)

steps.label: Priority Selection
steps.hint: Assign priorities A through E to the five categories. Priority A = highest investment.

steps.label: Allocate Attributes
steps.hint: Distribute your attribute points based on your priority selection.

steps.label: Allocate Skills
steps.hint: Distribute skill points based on your priority selection. Skill cap is typically 9 at character creation.

steps.label: Purchase Starting Gear
steps.hint: Spend your starting nuyen on weapons, armor, cyberware, and other equipment.
